_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Infected Insect Bites aligned to NHS Pharmacy First criteria including 48-hour worsening gate.
label: Infected Insect Bites

questions.text: When did the bite or sting happen?
Is the bite area red, warm, swollen, painful, or oozing?
Is the redness spreading beyond the immediate bite area?
Do you have fever (38°C or above) or feel systemically unwell?
Is there a red line tracking up from the bite toward your body?
Do you have difficulty breathing, swelling of the face or throat, or a severe rash all over your body?
Did symptoms clearly worsen 48 hours after the bite or sting?
Do you have confusion, severe shivering, very fast breathing, or other sepsis warning signs?

redFlags.message: These symptoms may indicate anaphylaxis. Call 999 immediately.

redFlags.description: Red line tracking from bite suggests lymphangitis
redFlags.message: A red line spreading from a bite needs emergency assessment. Call 999.
Spreading infection with fever needs same-day medical assessment.
Possible sepsis symptoms need emergency care now. Call 999.

eligibilityRules.condition: age >= 1 && q2 === true && q7 === true && q3 === false && q4 === false && q5 === false && q6 === false && q8 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Localised worsening infected bite (after 48 hours) without systemic or red-flag features
Under 1 year old requires GP review
Pharmacy infected-bite pathway requires clear worsening after 48 hours
Spreading redness or systemic features require GP or urgent review

outcomeRules.reason: No signs of infection — self-care appropriate
Localised infected insect bite — pharmacy treatment appropriate
Outside infected bite pharmacy criteria or exclusion factors present

selfCareAdvice: Clean the area with soap and water. Apply a cold compress. Avoid scratching. Antihistamine cream or oral antihistamine for itching. Monitor for spreading redness.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Flucloxacillin 500mg four times daily for 5–7 days
Clarithromycin 500mg twice daily for 5 days (penicillin allergy)
safetyNetAdvice: Return urgently if: redness spreads significantly, a red line appears, fever develops, or you feel very unwell.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: This pathway distinguishes local irritation from spreading infection; severe allergic reaction, necrotising infection, or rapid deterioration require emergency care — call 999 if in doubt.
